channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of Gaza and southern Lebanon on the eve of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon on Sunday in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region, while Palestinian officials said one strike on a mosque killed at least 19 people. A year after Hamas' Oct.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apathy dominates Tunisian election with President Saied expected to win with a challenger in prison
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — Voter apathy dominated Tunisia's presidential election Sunday, with a turnout of under 30% in the country's third presidential ballot since becoming the first nation to topple a dictator in last decade's Arab Spring.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli official warns 'everything is on the table' as IDF prepares response to Iranian missile attack
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant told Fox News on Sunday that the IDF is preparing a response to Iran's missile attacks, and said that "everything is on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolton blasts Biden for publicly opposing possible Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: Former White House national security adviser John Bolton sharply criticized the Biden administration Sunday for speaking publicly against the possibility of Israel striking nuclear sites or oil fields in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Over 6,000 people in Haiti leave their homes after gang attack killed dozens
item.description: The U.N.'s migration agency says nearly 6,300 people have fled their homes in the aftermath of an attack in central Haiti by heavily armed gang members that killed at least 70 people.
item.dc:creator: Pierre-Richard Luxama And Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Schiff supports Israeli strike on Iranian ballistic missile factories
item.description: Rep. Adam Schiff (D-Calif.), the leading candidate for his state's Senate seat, said he would advise Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to strike Iranian ballistic missile factories.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Under fire: Israel's Iron Dome winning but pressure's on as conflict with Iran escalates
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East intensifies, Iron Dome and Israel's other air defense systems have been continually challenged from prolonged use and evolving threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilians vote in tense local elections
item.description: Brazilians are voting in the first round of local elections for mayors, deputy mayors and councilors.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of northern Gaza, southern Beirut
item.description: Israel escalated its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Beirut amid concerns about an intensifying conflict in the region with Iran and its military proxy groups, including Hezbollah and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pope names 21 new cardinals, significantly increasing the pool who will one day elect his successor
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis named 21 new cardinals Sunday, significantly increasing the size of the College of Cardinals and further cementing his mark on the group of prelates who will one day elect his successor.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanese worldwide fear for their homeland and loved ones as violence escalates
item.description: It was a year ago when Jomana Siddiqui visited Lebanon, where her father was born — and is now buried. She planned to return there soon; this time, she thought, she would take her two teenage daughters. Instead, Siddiqui, who lives in California, now worries about relatives there.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A year into the Israel-Hamas war, students say a chill on free speech has reached college classrooms
item.description: Campuses across the U.S. have been calmer in the wake of last spring’s protests, yet many are mired in a lingering unease.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea and China mark their 75th anniversary of ties as outsiders question their relationship
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The leaders of North Korea and China marked the 75th anniversary of their diplomatic relations on Sunday by exchanging messages that expressed hopes for stronger ties, as outsiders raised questions about their relationship.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most Americans say Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider war: Survey
item.description: Most Americans said the Israel-Hamas war will lead to wider conflict in the region involving other countries, according to a survey released on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine downs a Russian warplane and Russia claims gains in the east
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian forces said they shot down a Russian fighter plane on Saturday while Russia claimed it made gains in Ukraine’s east.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US launches airstrikes by fighter jets and ships on Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military struck more than a dozen Houthi targets in Yemen on Friday, going after weapons systems, bases and other equipment belonging to the Iranian-backed rebels, U.S. officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clashes in Kenya as people discuss the deputy president's impeachment motion
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Supporters and opponents of Kenya's deputy president clashed Friday at public forums over an impeachment motion against him, which was introduced in parliament by the ruling alliance this week.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tunisia votes Sunday in its third presidential election since the Arab Spring
item.description: Tunisia's President Kais Saied faces few obstacles to winning another term in the country's presidential election Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked, state media reported Friday, after the South’s leader warned that Kim’s regime would collapse if he attempted to use nuclear arms.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military on Thursday warned people to evacuate communities in southern Lebanon that are outside a U.N.-declared buffer zone, signaling that it may widen a ground operation launched earlier this week against the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yazidi woman held hostage for 10 years in Gaza rescued in Israel, US operation
item.description: A Yazidi woman held captive by a Hamas terrorist after she was abducted by ISIS a decade ago has been returned to her family after she escaped through coordinated efforts by Israeli and U.S. officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel bans UN secretary-general over anti-Israel actions: 'Doesn't deserve to set foot on Israeli soil'
item.description: The United Nations has increasingly faced criticism over the past year for how officials have responded to and handled the conflicts in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel, Iran swap threats as strikes on Lebanon continue
item.description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has vowed to retaliate, saying Iran “made a big mistake" by launching a missile attack.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about fighting in Lebanon and Gaza
item.description: A day before Israel marks the one-year anniversary of the Hamas-led attack that killed 1,200 people, it ramped up strikes in the Gaza Strip and Lebanon, targeting Iran-backed militant groups.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 148 are dead in floods and landslides in Nepal
item.description: KATHMANDU, Nepal (AP) — Rescuers in Nepal recovered dozens of bodies from buses and other vehicles that were buried in landslides near the capital Kathmandu, as the death toll from flooding rose to at least 148 with dozens missing, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: BINAJ GURUBACHARYA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
